pr.title: Set mode `+x` on executable Python scripts
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Marked several Python entry-point scripts as executable by changing their file modes from 100644 to 100755. No source text changes, reviewer comments, or PR body were present in the slice.
what_changed: Changed configure.py file mode from 100644 to 100755.
Changed tools/download_tool.py file mode from 100644 to 100755.
Changed tools/find_include.py file mode from 100644 to 100755.
Changed tools/split_suggester.py file mode from 100644 to 100755.
Changed tools/transform_dep.py file mode from 100644 to 100755.
Changed tools/upload_progress.py file mode from 100644 to 100755.
The diff excerpt shows only mode changes and no line additions or deletions.
smart_moves: Kept the change narrowly scoped to executable permission bits for scripts that appear intended to be run directly.
Improved command-line usability for project tooling without changing script behavior.
decomp_lessons: Repository hygiene changes can matter for decomp workflows: executable Python tools should have mode 100755 when they are intended as direct commands.
For tooling-only PRs, mode-only diffs are valid evidence of a behavioral improvement at the shell/workflow level even when no source lines change.
When adding or touching project scripts such as configure.py or tools/*.py, verify both the shebang/entry-point intent and executable bit before merging.
follow_up_queries: Which Python scripts in the repository have shebangs but are not marked executable?
Are all directly invoked tools under tools/ mode 100755?
Do CI or setup docs assume running configure.py or tools/*.py directly?
